result:
Build succeeded cleanly (no new warnings). Here's what was implemented:

## Round 36: Mutual Information Network — Inter-Region Coupling Map

### What was added

**Data structures** (`life.c:313-340`):
- Grid partitioned into 20×10 blocks of 20×20 cells (200 blocks total)
- Top-40 strongest MI couplings stored in sorted list
- Per-cell overlay grid for Bresenham line rendering
- Stats: mean/max MI, network density, clustering coefficient

**Computation** (`life.c:3871-4082`):
- Extracts block population time series from timeline ring buffer (up to 256 frames)
- Quantizes populations into 8 bins per block for MI estimation
- Computes MI for all ~20K block pairs: `MI(X;Y) = Σ p(x,y) log₂(p(x,y)/(p(x)p(y)))`
- Maintains sorted top-N list during pair iteration
- Computes clustering coefficient from coupling graph adjacency
- Draws Bresenham lines between coupled block centers on overlay grid

**Rendering**:
- `cell_color()`: MI overlay shows coupling lines colored by strength (dark blue → cyan → green → yellow → magenta → white), faint block grid, dimmed cells underneath, block center markers
- Panel: 12-row panel showing max MI, mean MI, network density, clustering coefficient, top-5 couplings with block coordinates, and frame/block count
- Type 18 rendering for block grid lines in all zoom modes

**Key binding**: `@` (K was already taken by symmetry cycling)
- Toggles MI network on/off
- Lazy recompute every 8 generations while running
- Status bar shows `◈MI:value` when active
- Help bar includes `[@]mi`
